name: Chinchón, Colmenar de Oreja y Villaconejos – circular from Belmonte
name_original: Chinchón, Colmenar de Oreja y Villaconejos – circular desde Belmonte
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Belmonte De Tajo traverse a landscape characterized by open plains, extensive agricultural land, and gentle rolling hills. The region offers a blend of cultural exploration through picturesque historical villages and physical activity on its varied terrain. This area provides a network of routes suitable for different ability levels, connecting historical sites and natural features. Best touring cycling routes around Belmonte De Tajo The most popular touring cycling route is Chinchón, Colmenar de Oreja y Villaconejos – circular from Belmonte, an 18.7 miles (30.2 km) trail that takes 1 hour 51 minutes to complete. This moderate route passes through rural landscapes and villages. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Tajuña Greenway – Cantarranas Mill Hotel loop from Villarejo de Salvanés, a moderate 33.3 miles (53.6 km) path. This route follows the Tajuña Greenway, offering varied scenery. Local touring cyclists also love the Chinchón – Chinchón Castle loop from Villarejo de Salvanés, a 33.4 miles (53.8 km) trail leading through agricultural fields and past historical landmarks, often completed in about 4 hours 31 minutes. Touring cycling around Belmonte De Tajo is defined by open plains, agricultural fields, and historical villages. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more challenging paths. The routes in Belmonte De Tajo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 20 reviews. More than 500 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Belmonte De Tajo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Belmonte de Tajo?
A: There are over 85 touring cycling routes around Belmonte de Tajo, offering a wide variety of options for different skill levels and preferences. These routes traverse open plains, agricultural land, and historical villages.

Q: What kind of landscape can I expect while cycling in Belmonte de Tajo?
A: The landscape around Belmonte de Tajo is characterized by open plains, extensive agricultural land, and gentle rolling hills. You'll encounter picturesque historical villages and a blend of cultural exploration with physical activity. While the immediate area is plains, the broader Tajo region features river valleys and scattered woodlands.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Belmonte de Tajo?
A: The region is generally well-suited for cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures, making them ideal for touring. Summers can be hot, especially during midday, while winters are typically mild but can be cooler.

Q: Are there routes suitable for beginners or less experienced touring cyclists?
A: Yes, there are several routes suitable for beginners. Out of the 85 available routes, 6 are classified as easy, and 41 are moderate. These routes typically feature less elevation change and cover manageable distances, allowing for a relaxed cycling experience through the region's charming landscapes.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Many of the touring cycling routes around Belmonte de Tajo are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and end your journey in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e962913471/chinchon-colmenar-de-oreja-y-villaconejos-circular-from-belmonte" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chinchón, Colmenar de Oreja y Villaconejos – circular from Belmonte&lt;/a&gt; route is a moderate 18.7-mile (30.2 km) loop that passes through rural landscapes and villages.

Q: What historical sites or landmarks can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: The routes often connect historical towns and villages, allowing you to explore the region's cultural heritage. You might pass by the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2601433" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casasola Castle&lt;/a&gt;, or visit the charming town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5148900" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chinchón&lt;/a&gt; with its famous Plaza Mayor. Other notable sites include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2780457" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza Mayor of Colmenar de Oreja&lt;/a&gt; and various 16th-century churches and hermitages in the wider area.

Q: Are there any natural attractions to explore while cycling?
A: While cycling, you can discover natural features such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7115885" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Risco de las Cuevas of Perales de Tajuña&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7131675" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tagus River Riparian Forests&lt;/a&gt;. The region's landscape includes agricultural fields and scattered woodlands, providing a pleasant natural backdrop for your ride. You might also encounter natural monuments like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7148310" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Bermeja&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about cycling in Belmonte de Tajo?
A: The touring cycling routes around Belmonte de Tajo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 20 reviews. Cyclists often praise the blend of cultural exploration through historical villages and the physical activity offered by the varied terrain, as well as the open plains and agricultural scenery.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes in Belmonte de Tajo?
A: Yes, many of the moderate and easy routes are suitable for families. These paths often follow quieter roads through rural landscapes and villages, providing a safe and enjoyable experience for all ages. Look for routes with lower elevation gains and shorter distances for a comfortable family outing.

Q: Can I find cafes or places to rest along the touring cycling routes?
A: Many routes connect historical towns and villages, which typically offer opportunities for refreshments. Towns like Chinchón and Colmenar de Oreja, often featured on routes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e962913471/chinchon-colmenar-de-oreja-y-villaconejos-circular-from-belmonte" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chinchón, Colmenar de Oreja y Villaconejos – circular from Belmonte&lt;/a&gt;, have cafes and restaurants where you can take a break and refuel.

Q: Are there more challenging touring cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Yes, for experienced cyclists seeking a greater challenge, there are 38 difficult routes available. These routes feature significant elevation changes and longer distances. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20145474" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Morata de Tajuña Town Square – View of Morata de Tajuña loop from Villarejo de Salvanés&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 35 miles (56.4 km) with substantial climbs.

Q: How long do the touring cycling routes typically take to complete?
A: The duration varies significantly depending on the route's distance and difficulty. Moderate routes, like the 18.7-mile (30.2 km) &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e962913471/chinchon-colmenar-de-oreja-y-villaconejos-circular-from-belmonte" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chinchón, Colmenar de Oreja y Villaconejos – circular from Belmonte&lt;/a&gt;, can take around 1 hour 51 minutes. More challenging routes, such as the 33.4-mile (53.8 km) &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6911521" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chinchón – Chinchón Castle loop from Villarejo de Salvanés&lt;/a&gt;, might take over 4 hours.

Q: Is public transport available to access the cycling trails around Belmonte de Tajo?
A: Belmonte de Tajo is accessible by public transport, primarily bus services connecting it to larger towns and Madrid. While direct access to specific trailheads might require some planning, you can use public transport to reach the general area and then cycle to your desired starting point.
tips: Historic town of Madrid famous for its monumental arcaded Plaza Mayor, its limestone quarries (used in the Royal Palace) and its centuries-old wineries with D.O. Vinos de Madrid

This town, more than for its rabbits, is famous for its melons. Legend has it that a Zeluan soldier brought back from African lands the first seeds wrapped in cloth. It is true that at the end of the 16th century there are already records of the fame of the melons of this town.
The square is the axis of the city with its 15th century balcony houses and good restaurants around it. A marvel and a unique place in Spain.
